payload.answer: Brain DB is the source of truth; Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection; Slack is a strict memory intake agent. Telegram addition and cheapest high-performance model choice remain open questions.
citations: Brain DB should be the source of truth
Cognee should be a rebuildable semantic projection
Slack should be a strict memory intake agent
Open questions - Should Telegram be added later? Which model is cheapest while saturating performance?
payload.decision: Brain DB is the source of truth; Cognee should remain a rebuildable semantic projection; Slack should act as a strict memory intake agent. Open questions remain about adding Telegram later and choosing the cheapest model that still saturates performance.

entity_resolution.reason: No entity disambiguation was required from the provided summary.

payload.intent: Capture a durable architecture preference for the Brain system and note unresolved tooling/model questions.

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB should be the source of truth for memory.
Cognee should be a rebuildable semantic projection rather than the primary source of truth.
Slack should function as a strict memory intake agent.
Whether Telegram should be added later remains unresolved.
The cheapest model that still saturates performance has not yet been determined.

details: Preserved two unresolved questions as open.

repair_options: If the architecture changes, update the source-of-truth preference before syncing projections.
If Telegram is adopted, record it as a pending integration until confirmed.
If a model benchmark is available, store the selected model and the performance criterion used.
